title: ExcludeFromCodeCoverage attributes should include a justification
description: "ExcludeFromCodeCoverage" attributes should include a justificationAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbad-practiceWhy is this an issue?More InfoTheExcludeFromCodeCoverageAttributeis used to exclude portions of code fromcode coverage reporting. It is a bad practice to retain code that is not covered by unit tests. In .Net 5, theJustificationproperty was added to theExcludeFromCodeCoverageAttributeas an opportunity to document the rationale for the exclusion.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

compliant:
[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage(Justification = "Interop code that cannot be unit tested")]
public static extern int MessageBox(IntPtr hWnd, string text, string caption, uint type);

checks.message: ExcludeFromCodeCoverage attributes should include a Justification

logic.reasoning: Detects ExcludeFromCodeCoverage attributes without Justification parameter on methods and some class contexts. Framework may have limitations detecting all class-level attributes consistently. Excludes attributes that contain Justification parameter and test methods.
logic.self_critique_notes: Pattern detects attribute without parentheses or with empty parentheses. Excludes via pattern matching for 'Justification=' in source. Edge case: Multi-line attributes may need refinement.
